channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Turkish student at Tufts University is latest Palestinian supporter swept up in US crackdown
item.description: BOSTON (AP) — A Turkish student detained by federal officers as she walked along a street in a Boston suburb is the latest supporter of Palestinian causes to be swept up in the Trump administration’s crackdown on immigrants who express their political views.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL CASEY, JAKE OFFENHARTZ and KATHY McCORMACK, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Man accused of setting fire to Tesla vehicles in Las Vegas arrested, police say
item.description: LAS VEGAS (AP) — A man who set fire to Tesla vehicles in Las Vegas and who painted the word “resist” for authorities to find at the scene has been arrested.
item.dc:creator: RIO YAMAT, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: So long, Park City. Sundance Film Festival to relocate to Boulder, Colorado, in 2027
item.description: After a yearlong search, the Sundance Film Festival announced Thursday that its new home will be Boulder, Colorado, keeping Sundance in the mountains but moving it out of Park City, the Utah ski town that had for decades provided the premier independent film gathering its picturesque snowy backdrop.
item.dc:creator: JAKE COYLE, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: In-N-Out is raising prices on this secret menu workaround
item.description: Sorry, savvy In-N-Out Burger orderers, this menu loophole has been closed. According to a memorandum sent out to In-N-Out stores, and later posted to Reddit, the iconic burger chain is raising prices on individual slices of meat with cheese.
item.dc:creator: Austin Turner, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: How this auto union leader's support for Trump's tariffs scrambled labor politics again
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump has once again scrambled the politics of organized labor and the working class with his planned tariffs on auto imports.
item.dc:creator: BILL BARROW and STEPHEN GROVES, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Putin says US push for Greenland rooted in history, vows to uphold Russian interest in the Arctic
item.description: MOSCOW (AP) — Russia's President Vladimir Putin said Thursday that President Donald Trump's push for control over Greenland wasn't surprising given longtime U.S. interest in the mineral-rich territory.
item.dc:creator: VLADIMIR ISACHENKOV, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Judge orders government to preserve Signal messages about Houthi military strike
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — A federal judge on Thursday ordered the Trump administration to preserve records of a text message chat in which senior national security officials discussed sensitive details of plans for a U.S. military strike against Yemen’s Houthis. U.S.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL KUNZELMAN, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: First rain and then fire chase people from their homes in North and South Carolina
item.description: When Nicole Taylor and her family moved to their new home in the South Carolina mountains six months ago, the gorgeous view of Table Rock Mountain was the clincher.
item.dc:creator: JEFFREY COLLINS and BRITTANY PETERSON, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: The woman who killed Tejano music icon Selena in 1995 has been denied parole
item.description: The woman who has served nearly three decades in prison for killing Tejano music legend Selena Quintanilla-Pérez, has been denied parole by a Texas board.
item.dc:creator: Juan A. Lozano, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Texas man arrested in American Airlines groping incident, banned from flying on planes
item.description: A North Texas man is accused of groping a 22-year-old woman on an American Airlines flight last week, his third such accusation in recent years, according to court documents.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: What we know about the deadly air crash between a passenger jet and a US Army helicopter
item.description: A congressional hearing Thursday reinforced the idea that the January midair collision between an American Airlines passenger jet and an Army helicopter that killed all 67 people aboard both aircraft could have been prevented.
item.dc:creator: JOSH FUNK, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Luigi Mangione may get a laptop in jail to work on his defense in CEO murder case
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — A judge cleared the way Thursday for Luigi Mangione to get a laptop behind bars — if his jailers agree — so he can examine documents and other material in the UnitedHealthcare CEO murder case.
item.dc:creator: JENNIFER PELTZ and MICHAEL R. SISAK, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: New Jersey police officers accuse chief of turning department into 'animal house': docs
item.description: Police officers in North Bergen, New Jersey accuse their chief of creating an "animal house" where coffee pots were spiked with prescription drugs and open defecation was common.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: As many as 6 hurricanes forecast to strike US this season
item.description: “It only takes one landfall to create a devastating season,” AccuWeather chief meteorologist Jonathan Porter said in a statement.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Civil rights groups say push to dismantle Education Department will undo hard-won gains
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The rights of Americans to self-determine how to educate their children — a hotly contested matter that stretches back to at least the Civil War — have long been intertwined with the principle of equally and equitably educating children across racial lines.
item.dc:creator: MATT BROWN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Rubio says at least 300 foreign students' visas have been revoked
item.description: Secretary of State Marco Rubio said Thursday at least 300 foreign students have had their visas revoked amid the Trump administration's immigration agenda, a far higher number than was previously known.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, KTLA 5

item.title: Popular corn chips recalled over undeclared allergen
item.description: The popular chip brand Frito-Lay is recalling a limited number of Tostitos tortilla chips over an undeclared allergen.
item.dc:creator: Danielle Langenfeld, KTLA 5

item.title: White House withdrawing Stefanik nomination to serve as US ambassador to UN
item.description: The White House has informed the chair of the Senate Foreign Relations Committee that it is withdrawing Rep. Elise Stefanik’s (R-N.Y.) nomination to serve as U.S. ambassador to the United Nations, reflecting how critical her vote is in the House to passing President Trump’s agenda.
item.dc:creator: Emily Brooks, NewsNation

item.title: Prosecutors in the killings of 4 Idaho students seek to use man's crime scene paper as evidence
item.description: Prosecutors in the case of a man charged in the killings of four University of Idaho students are seeking to use as evidence a college paper he wrote that shows his knowledge of crime scenes, according to a court filing this week Bryan Kohberger, 30, is accused in the stabbing deaths of Ethan Chapin
item.dc:creator: ADRIAN SAINZ, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Congress questions the FAA, US Army and NTSB over deadly midair collision in DC
item.description: The head of the National Transportation Safety Board appeared alongside officials from the Federal Aviation Administration and the Army's aviation division at a hearing on Thursday.
item.dc:creator: Josh Funk, Fox 5

item.title: Asian Americans and Pacific Islanders are still targets of hatred 5 years after pandemic surge
item.description: Up until 2020, Anna Wong had gone her entire adulthood in Los Angeles without ever facing blatantly racist abuse for being Asian. After COVID-19 hit, she was accosted twice in six months.
item.dc:creator: TERRY TANG, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Florida considers bill to roll back child labor laws to fill jobs once held by undocumented migrants
item.description: Florida lawmakers are considering major changes to state child labor laws to address a potential shortage of migrant workers due to GOP-led immigration crackdowns, which would allow teens to work longer hours and earn lower wages.
item.dc:creator: Elizabeth Crisp, KRON4

item.title: New Social Security clawback policy starts this week: What to know
item.description: A major change in how the Social Security Administration (SSA) treats its overpayments is set to kick in Thursday.
item.dc:creator: Jeremy Tanner, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Chance of Laken Riley's illegal immigrant killer doing time in El Salvador prison addressed by lawmakers
item.description: Could Jose Ibarra, Laken Riley's convicted killer, be deported to an El Salvador prison where the Trump administration just sent hundreds of Venezuelan gang members?
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Texas man flees scene after crashing car at 100 mph, leaving his critically injured family behind: police
item.description: Police are searching for a Texas man who crashed his car while driving at 100 mph and fled the scene leaving his critically injured family in the ditch, police say
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Pilot rescued with 2 girls from icy Alaska lake was not authorized to have passengers, official says
item.description: ANCHORAGE, Alaska (AP) — A pilot rescued with two young family members after they survived a night on the wing of an airplane partially submerged in an Alaska lake is a student pilot who was not authorized to fly with passengers, according to a U.S. official and federal aviation records.
item.dc:creator: MARK THIESSEN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Washington researchers warn of serious decline in honey bee colonies in 2025
item.description: Losses of commercial honey bee colonies are projected to be the highest on record in the last decade, according to Washington State University researchers.
item.dc:creator: Jenna Deml, KRON4

item.title: A heart-shaped note was found in socks bound for Luigi Mangione
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Someone tucked a heart-shaped note of encouragement into socks packed for Luigi Mangione to wear to court last month in the UnitedHealthcare CEO murder case, according to court documents made public Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: JENNIFER PELTZ and MICHAEL R. SISAK, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Stepmother accused of holding man captive for over 20 years appears in court
item.description: Kimberly Sullivan arrived at Waterbury Superior Court just before 10 a.m., where she was expected to plead not guilty to kidnapping and assault charges, according to her lawyers. But the case was continued until Friday.
item.dc:creator: Jenn Brink, KTLA 5

item.title: Inflatable baby protest highlights child care budget gap
item.description: Activists and lawmakers rallied outside Gov. Kathy Hochul’s Albany office with a 15-foot inflatable baby to protest severe gaps in child care funding.
item.dc:creator: Johan Sheridan, NewsNation

item.title: Appeals court halts Chutkan's order for DOGE records
item.description: A federal appeals court on Wednesday temporarily blocked an order from U.S. District Judge Tanya Chutkan directing Elon Musk and the Department of Government Efficiency (DOGE) to turn over records and answer questions about its efforts to slash the federal bureaucracy. A three-judge panel on the U.
item.dc:creator: Ella Lee, Fox 5

item.title: Appeals court won't lift Boasberg's order blocking Alien Enemies Act
item.description: A federal appeals court in a 2-1 decision Wednesday refused to lift U.S. District Judge James Boasberg’s order blocking the Trump administration from swiftly deporting migrants under the Alien Enemies Act. The Justice Department had urged the three-judge panel on the U.S.
item.dc:creator: Zach Schonfeld, Fox 5

item.title: Babysitter checking for monsters finds man hiding under child's bed
item.description: A 27-year-old man has been arrested after a Kansas babysitter found him hiding under a child's bed, police said. At around 10:30 p.m.
item.dc:creator: Wil Day, KTLA 5

item.title: Doctor accused of trying to kill his wife during hike
item.description: Police say the man tried to push his wife off the trail and allegedly hit her in the head multiple times with a rock.
item.dc:creator: Jill Kuramoto, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Atlantic publishes Trump Cabinet group chat messages
item.description: The Atlantic has published the Signal group chat messages among national security leaders that were inadvertently shared with Editor-in-Chief Jeffrey Goldberg, noting administration officials said Tuesday they were not classified.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, WFLA, NBC

item.title: GOP-led states push for control of school aid as Trump promises a smaller federal role in education
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Governors in several Republican-led states are pressing the Trump administration to cut strings attached to their federal education money, a goal conservatives have long dreamed of that now appears within reach as President Donald Trump moves to dismantle the Education Department.
item.dc:creator: COLLIN BINKLEY, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Tennessee pitcher Karlyn Pickens throws fastest pitch in college softball history
item.description: Tennessee softball standout Karlyn Pickens wrote her name into the history books on Monday with a record-breaking pitch.
item.dc:creator: Gregory Raucoules, WGN 9
